[![Logo](docs/logo/logo_small.png)](https://constellation.pages.desy.de/)

# Constellation

## The Autonomous Control and Data Acquisition System for Dynamic Experimental Setups

Constellation is a control and data acquisition system for small-scale experiments and experimental setup with volatile and dynamic constituents such as testbeam environments or laboratory test stands. Constellation aims to provide a flexible SCADA software system that requires minimal effort for the integration of new devices, that is based on widely adopted open-source network communication libraries and that keeps the required maintenance as low as possible.

More details about the project can be found on the website at [constellation.pages.desy.de](https://constellation.pages.desy.de/).

[![Latest Release](https://gitlab.desy.de/constellation/constellation/-/badges/release.svg)](https://gitlab.desy.de/constellation/constellation/-/releases)

[![REUSE Status](https://api.reuse.software/badge/gitlab.desy.de/constellation/constellation)](https://api.reuse.software/info/gitlab.desy.de/constellation/constellation)
[![Coverage Report](https://gitlab.desy.de/constellation/constellation/badges/main/coverage.svg)](https://gitlab.desy.de/constellation/constellation/-/graphs/main/charts)

documentation:
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024 DESY and the Constellation authors
SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-4.0

Serial interface for a Keithley Series 2400 SourceMeter as voltage source.

Keithley needs to be set to RS-232 mode with:
- BAUD: 19200
- BITS: 8
- PARITY: EVEN
- TERMINATOR: <CR+LF>
- FLOW_CONTROL: NONE

modules.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470
documentation:
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024 DESY and the Constellation authors
SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-4.0

This module provides the classes for communication with CAEN's NDT1470 desktop
HV power supplies.

Supported are USB or TCP/IP and, for compatibility, the pycaenhv API for CAEN's
HV crate series is used whenever possible.

functions.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.status_unpack
documentation: decodes status bits (see p25 of the NDT1470 user manual (UM2027, rev.17). )

functions.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.alarm_unpack
documentation:
Decodes board alarm status bits (see p26 of the NDT1470 user manual
(UM2027, rev.17). )

documentation: decodes error message

methods.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.CaenDecode.val
documentation: decodes values returned from the device

Decodes the string sent back by the NDT1470. Based on information on p24
of the NDT1470 user manual (UM2027, rev.17).

methods.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.CaenNDT1470Manager.command
documentation:
Set values for module 'bd', channel 'ch' and parameter 'par' to value 'val'.

If value is 'None', then the value will be requested
Module commands have an empty channel number.

NOTE: The signature of this method differs from the pycaenhv one.

methods.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.CaenNDT1470Manager._send_cmd
documentation: constructs and sends cmd to device. Wraps result into a CaenDecode object.

methods.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.CaenNDT1470Manager._send_raw
documentation:
Sends a (raw) command to the device. `msg' is a string that will be
byte-encoded before sending. From the NDT1470 user manual (UM2027,
rev.17, p24) :

The Format of a command string is the following :
$BD:**,CMD:***,CH*,PAR:***,VAL:***.**<CR, LF >
The fields that form the command are :
BD : 0..31 module address (to send the command)
CMD : MON, SET
CH : 0..NUMCH (NUMCH=2 for N1570, NUMCH=4 for the other modules)
PAR : (see parameters tables)
VAL : (numerical value must have a Format compatible with resolution and range)

methods.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.CaenNDT1470Manager._receive_raw
documentation: receives (raw) response from last issued command to the device.

methods.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.CaenNDT1470Manager.__enter__
documentation: Acquire the lock to prevent the other threads from interfering.

methods.name: constellation.satellites.CaenHV.lib_caen_ndt1470.CaenNDT1470Manager.__exit__
documentation: Release the lock to allow other threads access.

Class for interacting with a CAEN NDT1470 NIM HV module.

Supports ethernet (telnet) interface or serial (USB) connections.

methods.name: constellation.satellites.Mariner.Mariner.Mariner.do_initializing
documentation:
Configure the Satellite and any associated hardware.

The configuration is provided as Configuration object which works
similar to a regular dictionary but tracks access to its keys. If a key
does not exist or if one exists but is not used, the Satellite will
automatically return an error or warning, respectively.

documentation:
The main run routine.

Here, the main part of the mission would be performed.

methods.name: constellation.satellites.Mariner.Mariner.Mariner.get_attitude
documentation:
Determine and return the space craft's attitude.

This is an example for a command that can be triggered from a Controller
via CSCP. The return value of the function consists of a message, a
payload value and an (optional) dictionary with meta information.
Example for a Satellite class.

modules.name: constellation.core.commandmanager
documentation:
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024 DESY and the Constellation authors
SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-4.0

This module provides classes for managing CSCP requests/replies within
Constellation Satellites.

functions.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.cscp_requestable
documentation:
Register a function as a supported command for CSCP.

See CommandReceiver for a description of the expected signature.

functions.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.get_cscp_commands
documentation: Loop over all class methods and return those marked as CSCP commands.

classes.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver

methods.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver.__init__
documentation: Initialize the Receiver and set up a ZMQ REP socket on given port.

methods.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver._add_com_thread
documentation: Add the command receiver thread to the communication thread pool.

methods.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver._recv_cmds
documentation: Request receive loop.

methods.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver.get_commands
documentation:
Return all commands supported by the Satellite.

No payload argument.

This will include all methods with the @cscp_requestable decorator. The
doc string of the function will be used to derive the summary and
payload argument description for each command by using the first and the
second line of the doc string, respectively (not counting empty lines).

methods.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver.get_type
documentation:
Return the type of the Satellite.

No payload argument.

methods.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver.get_name
documentation:
Return the canonical name of the Satellite.

No payload argument.

methods.name: constellation.core.commandmanager.CommandReceiver.shutdown
documentation:
Queue the Satellite's reentry.

No payload argument.

Class for handling incoming CSCP requests.

Commands will call specific methods of the inheriting class which should
have the following signature:

def COMMAND(self, request: cscp.CSCPMessage) -> (str, any, dict):

The expected return values are:
- reply message (string)
- payload (any)
- map (dictionary) (e.g. for meta information)

Inheriting classes need to decorate such command methods with
'@cscp_requestable' to make them callable through CSCP requests.

If a method

def _COMMAND_is_allowed(self, request: cscp.CSCPMessage) -> bool:

exists, it will be called first to determine whether the command is
currently allowed or not.

documentation: Return a dictionary of Events triggered for failed Satellites.

methods.name: constellation.core.heartbeatchecker.HeartbeatChecker.get_failed
documentation: Get a list of the names of all failed Satellites.

methods.name: constellation.core.heartbeatchecker.HeartbeatChecker._run
documentation: Run background thread performing the heartbeat checking.

methods.name: constellation.core.heartbeatchecker.HeartbeatChecker.start
documentation: Start the heartbeat checking for a single Satellite.

methods.name: constellation.core.heartbeatchecker.HeartbeatChecker.start_all
documentation: Start the heartbeat checking all registered Satellites.

methods.name: constellation.core.heartbeatchecker.HeartbeatChecker.stop
documentation: Stop heartbeat checking.

Checks periodically Satellites' state via subscription to its Heartbeat.

Individual heartbeat checks run in separate threads. In case of a failure
(either the Satellite is in ERROR/SAFE state or has missed several
heartbeats) the corresponding thread will set a `failed` event.
Alternatively, an action can be triggered via a callback.

documentation:
Schedule a callback at regular intervals.

The callable needs to return a value [any] and a unit [str] and take no
arguments. If you have a callable that requires arguments, consider
using functools.partial to fill in the necessary information at
scheduling time.

Sender mixin class for Constellation Monitoring Distribution Protocol.

Any method of inheriting classes that has the @schedule_metric decorator,
will be regularly polled for new values and a corresponding Metric be sent
on the monitoring port.

documentation: Return Satellite to Initialized state.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite._wrap_stop
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'stopping' transitional state of the FSM.

This method performs the basic Satellite transition before passing
control to the device-specific public method.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite.do_stopping
documentation: Stop the data acquisition.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite._wrap_start
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'run' state of the FSM.

This method performs the basic Satellite transition before passing
control to the device-specific public method.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite.do_starting
documentation: Final preparation for acquisition.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite.do_run
documentation:
The acquisition event loop.

This method will be started by the Satellite and run in a thread. It
therefore needs to monitor the self.stop_running Event and close itself
down if the Event is set.

NOTE: This method is not inherently thread-safe as it runs in the
context of the Satellite and can modify data accessible to the main
thread. However, the state machine can effectively act as a lock and
prevent competing access to the same objects while in RUNNING state as
long as care is taken in the implementation.

The state machine itself uses the RTC model by default (see
https://python-statemachine.readthedocs.io/en/latest/processing_model.html?highlight=thread)
which should make the transitions themselves safe.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite._wrap_failure
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'ERROR' state of the FSM.

This method performs the basic Satellite transition before passing
control to the device-specific public method.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite.fail_gracefully
documentation: Method called when reaching 'ERROR' state.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite._wrap_interrupt
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'interrupting' transitional state of the FSM.

This method performs the basic Satellite transition before passing
control to the device-specific public method.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite.do_interrupting
documentation:
Interrupt data acquisition and move to Safe state.

Defaults to calling the stop and land handlers.

methods.name: constellation.core.satellite.Satellite._thread_exception
documentation:
Handle exceptions in threads.

Change state to FAULT.

Intended to be installed as threading.excepthook.

documentation:

Additional version information may be included in the meta map (final

methods.name: constellation.core.broadcastmanager.CHIRPBroadcaster._discover_service
documentation: Add a service to internal list and possibly queue a callback.

methods.name: constellation.core.broadcastmanager.CHIRPBroadcaster._depart_service
documentation: Depart with a service.

methods.name: constellation.core.broadcastmanager.CHIRPBroadcaster._run
documentation: Start listening in on broadcast

Manages service discovery and broadcast via the CHIRP protocol.

Listening and reacting to CHIRP broadcasts is implemented in a dedicated
thread that can be started after the class has been instantiated.

Discovered services are added to an internal cache. Callback methods can be
registered either by calling register_request() or by using the
@chirp_callback() decorator. The callback will be added to the satellite's
internal task queue once the corresponding service has been offered by other
satellites via broadcast.

Offered services can be registered via register_offer() and are announced on
incoming request broadcasts or via broadcast_offers().

modules.name: constellation.core.chirp
documentation:
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024 DESY and the Constellation authors
SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-4.0

Module implementing the Constellation Host Identification and Reconnaissance Protocol (CHIRP).

functions.name: constellation.core.chirp.get_uuid
documentation: Return the UUID for a string using MD5 hashing.

classes.name: constellation.core.chirp.CHIRPServiceIdentifier
documentation:
Identifies the type of service.

The CONTROL service identifier indicates a CSCP (Constellation Satellite
Control Protocol) service.

The HEARTBEAT service identifier indicates a CHP (Constellation Heartbeat
Protocol) service.

The MONITORING service identifier indicates a CMDP (Constellation Monitoring
Distribution Protocol) service.

The DATA service identifier indicates a CDTP (Constellation Data
Transmission Protocol) service.

The NONE identifier is used for initialization only, and is not a valid
service type.

classes.name: constellation.core.chirp.CHIRPMessageType
documentation:
Identifies the type of message sent or received via the CHIRP protocol.

See docs/protocols/chirp.md for details.

REQUEST: A message with REQUEST type indicates that CHIRP hosts should reply
with an OFFER

OFFER: A message with OFFER type indicates that service is available

DEPART: A message with DEPART type indicates that a service is no longer
available

NONE: Value used for initialization only, not a valid message type.

documentation: Initialize and configure the satellite.

documentation: Close all open sockets.

methods.name: constellation.core.datareceiver.DataReceiver.do_run
documentation:
Handle the data enqueued by the ZMQ Poller.

The implementation of this method will have to monitor the
`self._state_thread_evt` Event and perform all necessary steps for
stopping the acquisition when `self._state_thread_evt.is_set()` is
`True` as there will be *no* call to `do_stopping` in this class. This
allows to e.g. wrap opening files in `with ...` or `try: ... finally:
...` clauses.

This method will be executed in a separate thread by the underlying
Satellite class. It therefore needs to monitor the self.stop_running
Event and close itself down if the Event is set.

documentation: Get optional playload for the end-of-run event (EOR).

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender.EOR
documentation: Set optional playload for the end-of-run event (EOR).

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender.BOR
documentation: Get optional playload for the beginning-of-run event (BOR).

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender.BOR
documentation: Set optional playload for the beginning-of-run event (BOR).

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender._wrap_launch
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'launching' transitional state of the FSM.

This method starts the PushThread for the DataSender.

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender._wrap_land
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'landing' transitional state of the FSM.

This method will stop the PushThread.

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender._wrap_start
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'run' state of the FSM.

This method notifies the data queue of the beginning and end of the data run,
as well as performing basic satellite transitioning.

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender._wrap_stop
documentation:
Wrapper for the 'stopping' transitional state of the FSM.

Sends the EOR event after base class wrapper and `do_stopping` have
finished.

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSender.do_run
documentation:
Perform the data acquisition and enqueue the results.

This is only an abstract method. Inheriting classes must implement their
own acquisition method.

This method will be executed in a separate thread by the underlying
Satellite class. It therefore needs to monitor the self.stop_running
Event and close itself down if the Event is set.

If you want to transmit a payload as part of the end-of-run event (BOR),
set the corresponding value via the `BOR` property before leaving this
method.

This method should return a string that will be used for setting the
Status once the data acquisition is finished.
Constellation Satellite which pushes data via ZMQ.

classes.name: constellation.core.datasender.RandomDataSender

methods.name: constellation.core.datasender.RandomDataSender.do_run
documentation: Example implementation that generates random values.
Constellation Satellite which pushes RANDOM data via ZMQ.

classes.name: constellation.core.datasender.DataSenderArgumentParser
documentation: Customized Argument parser providing DataSender-specific options.

This module provides a base class for Constellation Satellite modules.

documentation: Close down connections and perform orderly re-entry.

classes.name: constellation.core.base.ConstellationArgumentParser
documentation: Customized Argument parser providing basic Satellite options.

classes.name: constellation.core.base.ConstellationLogger

methods.name: constellation.core.base.ConstellationLogger.__init__
documentation: Init logger and define extra levels.

methods.name: constellation.core.base.ConstellationLogger.trace
documentation:
Define level for verbose information which allows to follow the call
stack of the host program.

methods.name: constellation.core.base.ConstellationLogger.status
documentation:
Define level for important information about the host program to the
end user with low frequency.

methods.name: constellation.core.base.ConstellationLogger.error
documentation: Map error level to CRITICAL.

Custom Logger class for Constellation.

Defines the following log levels:

- logging.NOTSET : 0
- logging.TRACE : 5
- logging.DEBUG : 10
- logging.INFO : 20
- logging.STATUS : 25
- logging.WARNING : 30
- logging.ERROR : mapped to CRITICAL
- logging.CRITICAL : 50

classes.name: constellation.core.base.BaseSatelliteFrame

methods.name: constellation.core.base.BaseSatelliteFrame._add_com_thread
documentation:
Method to add a background communication service thread to the pool.

Does nothing in the base class.

methods.name: constellation.core.base.BaseSatelliteFrame._start_com_threads
documentation: Start all background communication threads.

methods.name: constellation.core.base.BaseSatelliteFrame._stop_com_threads
documentation: Stop all background communication threads within timeout [s].

methods.name: constellation.core.base.BaseSatelliteFrame.reentry
documentation: Orderly destroy the satellite.

Base class for all Satellite components to inherit from.

Provides the basic internal Satellite infrastructure related to logging, ZMQ
and threading that all Satellite communication service components (i.e.
mixin classes) share.

modules.name: constellation.core.fsm
documentation:
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024 DESY and the Constellation authors
SPDX-License-Identifier: CC-BY-4.0
!/usr/bin/env python3

classes.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteState
documentation: Available states to cycle through.

classes.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteFSM

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteFSM.before_transition
documentation: Set status before the state change.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteFSM.after_transition
documentation: Set flag indicating state change.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteFSM.write_diagram
documentation: Create a png with the FSM schematic.
Manage the satellite's state and its transitions.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.initialize
documentation:
Initiate 'initialize' state transition via a CSCP request.

Takes dictionary with configuration values as argument.

If the transition is not allowed, TransitionNotAllowed will be thrown by
the FSM.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.launch
documentation:
Initiate launch state transition via a CSCP request.

No payload argument.

If the transition is not allowed, TransitionNotAllowed will be thrown by
the FSM.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.land
documentation:
Initiate landing state transition via a CSCP request.

No payload argument.

If the transition is not allowed, TransitionNotAllowed will be thrown by
the FSM.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.start
documentation:
Initiate start state transition via a CSCP request.

Payload: run identifier [str].

If the transition is not allowed, TransitionNotAllowed will be thrown by
the FSM.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.stop
documentation:
Initiate stop state transition via a CSCP request.

No payload argument.

If the transition is not allowed, TransitionNotAllowed will be thrown by
the FSM.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.reconfigure
documentation:
Initiate reconfigure state transition via a CSCP request.

Takes dictionary with configuration values as argument.

If the transition is not allowed, TransitionNotAllowed will be thrown by
the FSM.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.interrupt
documentation:
Initiate interrupt state transition via a CSCP request.

No payload argument.

If the transition is not allowed, TransitionNotAllowed will be thrown by
the FSM.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.failure
documentation:
Enter error state transition via a CSCP request.

No payload argument.

This is intended for debugging purposes only and should not be called in
normal operation.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler._transition
documentation:
Prepare and enqeue a transition task.

The task consists of the respective transition method and the request
payload as argument.

If thread is true, then the transition method will be run as a separate
thread. If allowed by the FSM, threads can potentially be interrupted by
a subsequent task such as 'RUN' can be stopped via the 'stop' command.

Otherwise, the transition will be run in the main satellite thread and
subsequent tasks are queued and blocked until the original transition
has finished.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler._start_transition
documentation: Start a transition and advance FSM for transitional states.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler._start_transition_thread
documentation: Start a transition thread with the given fcn and arguments.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler._state_transition_thread_complete
documentation: Callback method when a transition thread is done.

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.get_state
documentation:
Return the current state of the Satellite.

No payload argument.

Payload of the response contains 'last_changed'

methods.name: constellation.core.fsm.SatelliteStateHandler.get_status
documentation:
Get a string describing the current status of the Satellite.

No payload argument.

Defines the message types of the CDTP.

Part of the Constellation Satellite Data Protocol, see
docs/protocols/cdtp.md for details.

classes.name: constellation.core.cdtp.CDTPMessage

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.CDTPMessage.set_header
documentation: Sets information retrieved from a message header.

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.CDTPMessage.__str__
documentation: Pretty-print request.
Class holding details of a received CDTP command.

classes.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter.__init__
documentation:
Initialize transmitter.

socket: the ZMQ socket to use if no other is specified on send()/recv()
calls.

name: the name to use in the message header.

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter.send_start
documentation:
Send starting message of data run over a ZMQ socket.

Follows the Constellation Data Transmission Protocol.

payload: meta information about the beginning of run.

flags: additional ZMQ socket flags to use during transmission.

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter.send_data
documentation:
Send data message of data run over a ZMQ socket.

Follows the Constellation Data Transmission Protocol.

payload: meta information about the beginning of run.

meta: optional dictionary that is sent as a map of string/value
pairs with the header.

flags: additional ZMQ socket flags to use during transmission.

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter.send_end
documentation:
Send ending message of data run over a ZMQ socket.

Follows the Constellation Data Transmission Protocol.

payload: meta information about the end of run.

flags: additional ZMQ socket flags to use during transmission.

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter.recv
documentation:
Receive a multi-part data transmission.

Follows the Constellation Data Transmission Protocol.

flags: additional ZMQ socket flags to use during transmission.

Returns: CTDPMessage

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter.decode
documentation: Decode a binary message into a CTDPMessage.

methods.name: constellation.core.cdtp.DataTransmitter._dispatch
documentation:
Dispatch CDTP message.

msgtype: flag identifying whether transmitting beginning-of-run, data or end-of-run

payload: data to send.

meta: dictionary to include in the map of the message header.

flags: additional ZMQ socket flags to use during transmission.

Returns: None
Base class for sending Constellation data packets via ZMQ.
